---
title: "Salmon Biphasic model v1"
author: "Viktor Thunell" 
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%d %B, %Y')`"
format: 
  html:
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show code"
    page-layout: full
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
knitr: 
  opts_chunk:
    fig.align: center
    out-width: 80%
editor: source
execute: 
  echo: true
  eval: true
  cache: true
---


```{r}
# for the exponential I need to specify rate and the inverse of the mean or a fraction of he mean concentrates mass below the mean.
ggplot() +
   stat_function(fun = dexp, args = list(rate = 1/(200)), xlim = c(0,1000))
mean(rexp(10000, rate = 1/(139/5)) < 139)

l.lsd <- sqrt(log(1 + (278^2) / (1378^2)))
l.lmean <- log(1378) - 0.5 * l.lsd^2
k.lsd <- sqrt(log(1 + (0.28^2) / (0.43^2)))
k.lmean <- log(0.43) - 0.5 * k.lsd^2  

dlnorm(1000, meanlog = l.lmean, sdlog = l.lsd)
ggplot() +
   stat_function(fun = dlnorm, args = list(meanlog = l.lmean, sdlog = l.lsd), xlim = c(0,10000))
ggplot() +
   stat_function(fun = dlnorm, args = list(meanlog = k.lmean, sdlog = k.lsd), xlim = c(0,10000))

g.lsd <- sqrt(log(1 + (10^2) / (50^2))) # sqrt of variance (= sd) of the lognormal distr. 50 and 10 is arbitrary atm.
ggplot() +
   stat_function(fun = dlnorm, args = list(meanlog = log(g.lsd), sdlog = 0.1), xlim = c(0,1))
1 / sqrt(10)
                 
ggplot() +
   stat_function(fun = dlnorm, args = list(meanlog = log(0.43), sdlog = log(1.1)), xlim = c(0,100))

ggplot() +
   stat_function(fun = dnorm, args = list(mean = 2, sd = 0.5), xlim = c(0,4))


exp(log(k.lsd))
lnorm(1,log(k.lsd), sd = 0.3)
ggplot() +
   stat_function(fun = dnorm, args = list(mean = log(k.lsd), sd = 10), xlim = c(0,30)) 
  


 rgamma(5, shape = 2, scale = 1)/
# #   as_tibble() %>%
# ggplot() +
#   #ggplot(aes(x= value)) +
#   #geom_density(fill = "lightblue", alpha = 0.5) +
#   stat_function(fun = dgamma, args = list(shape = 2, rate = 1), xlim = c(0,10))
# 
# # sd lower than 1 means 
# ggplot() +
#   stat_function(fun = dlnorm, args = list(meanlog = log(0.5), sdlog = 1),xlim = c(0,1)) +
#   geom_vline(xintercept = 0.5)
# 
ggplot() +
  #stat_function(fun = dlnorm, args = list(meanlog = log(100), sdlog = 10), xlim = c(0,400))
stat_function(fun = dnorm, args = list(mean = 250, sd = 50), xlim = c(0,500))
```
```{r}
sallaa %>%
  filter(age.type == "both") %>%
  ggplot() +
  geom_density(aes(juv.age, color = spat.unit)) +
  facet_wrap(~spat.unit)
# for poisson for smotl age
pmf <- dpois(0:15, lambda = 1.5)

ggplot(data.frame(0:15, pmf), aes(0:15, pmf)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.8, fill = "steelblue") +
  labs(x = "x", y = "P(X = x)", title = "Poisson(λ = 2)") +
  theme_minimal()

ggplot() +
  stat_function(fun = dgamma, args = list(shape = 1.5, rate = 1/.36), xlim = c(0,6)) +
  stat_function(fun = dexp, args = list(rate = 1/0.36), xlim = c(0,6), color = "red")

dgamma(c(1:4), shape = 25, scale = 0.1)
```


```{r}
dg<-rgamma(6, shape = 25, scale = 0.1)
x=dg/sum(dg)

rcat(10, x)
```


```{r}

ggplot() +
   stat_function(fun = dlnorm, args = list(meanlog = log(139), sdlog = 1), xlim = c(0,500)) +
   stat_function(fun = dlnorm, args = list(meanlog = 139, sdlog = 1), xlim = c(0,500), color = "red")

# gamma w mean = 2 makes shape = 2*rate and variance = 2/rate. With sd = 0.5, variance = 0.25, rate=8 and shape 16.
# gamma w mean = 2 makes shape = 2*rate and variance = 2/rate. With sd = 1.5, variance = 1.5^2, rate=8 and shape 16.
ggplot() +
  stat_function(fun = dgamma, args = list(shape = 25, scale = 0.1), xlim = c(0,6)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 2) +
  stat_function(fun = dgamma, args = list(shape = 16, rate = 8), xlim = c(0,15), color = "red")
```


```{r}
k_lsd <- sqrt(log(1 + (0.28^2) / (0.43^2))) # sqrt of variance
k_lmean <- log(0.43) - 0.5 * k_lsd^2  

set.seed(1)
draws <- rlnorm(1e5, meanlog = mean_log, sdlog = sd_log)

# quick checks
mean(draws)   # ~0.43
sd(draws)     # ~0.28
quantile(draws, c(0.025, 0.5, 0.975))

# plot density with quantiles
library(ggplot2)

q <- quantile(draws, c(0.025, 0.5, 0.975))

ggplot(data.frame(x = draws), aes(x)) +
  geom_density(fill = "skyblue", alpha = 0.4) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = q, linetype = c("dashed", "solid", "dashed"),
             color = c("red", "black", "red")) +
  labs(
    title = "Lognormal prior (mean ≈ 0.43, sd ≈ 0.28 on original scale)",
    x = "Value", y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)
```

```{r}
badNodes <- character()
for(n in stochNodes){
  # calculate only this node (isolates its contribution)
  lp <- try(biph8.model$calculate(n), silent = TRUE)
  # try to fetch its log-prob
  lpNode <- try(biph8.model$getLogProb(n), silent = TRUE)
  if(inherits(lpNode, "try-error")) {
    cat("can't get logprob for", n, "\n")
  } else {
    if(is.infinite(lpNode) || is.nan(lpNode) || lpNode < -1e8) badNodes <- c(badNodes, n)
  }
}
cat("Suspect nodes (very small or -Inf):\n"); print(badNodes)
biph8.model$length_mm[10905]

```
